title: GCP ML Engineer Knowledge Test
description: Test your understanding of GCP ML Engineer with 25 questions.

questions.question: Design a beginner friendly GCP ML pipeline to train a sentiment classifier on customer support chats with privacy and explainability in mind. Ingest chat streams via Pub/Sub, Dataflow cleans and redacts PII, writes raw data to Cloud Storage and de-identified aggregates to BigQuery. Train weekly in Vertex AI, deploy an online endpoint with Explainable AI enabled, log per-prediction attributions to BigQuery, and implement a simple drift check on attribution distributions with rollback to the previous model if drift is detected. Include rough cost notes?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked

Tests ability to design a privacy conscious, explainable ML pipeline using GCP tools, plus a simple drift-driven rollback.

## Key Concepts

- Pub/Sub streaming ingestion
- Dataflow ETL with PII redaction
- BigQuery as attribution store
- Vertex AI Explainable AI for interpretability
- Drift detection via attribution distributions
- Rollback strategy to prior model version

## Code Example

```javascript
// Pseudo config sketch
const trainJob = {
  type: 'VertexAI Training',
  schedule: 'weekly',
  model: 'text-classifier',
  explainability: true
}
```

## Follow-up Questions

- How would you test explainability to meet privacy rules?
- How would you scale drift checks as data grows?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
This question tests end-to-end knowledge of the GCP ML stack, including Pub/Sub for ingestion, Dataflow for schema validation and batching, Data Labeling Service for human-in-the-loop labeling, Cloud Storage for data persistence, BigQuery for provenance tracking and metrics, Vertex AI for training and deployment, and canary deployments with governance thresholds.

## Key Concepts
- Iterative data labeling loops with versioning
- Multilingual text preprocessing and model training in Vertex AI
- End-to-end data lineage and governance
- Canary deployment strategies with automated rollback

## Code Example
```python
from google.cloud import aiplatform

aiplatform.init(project='my-project', location='us-central1')

job = aiplatform.CustomTrainingJob(
    display_name='sentiment-classifier',
    script_path='train.py',
    container_uri='us-docker.pkg.dev/vertex-ai/training/pytorch-cpu.1-13'
)

model = job.run(
    dataset=training_dataset,
    model_display_name='sentiment-classifier-v2',
    epochs=10,
    replica_count=1
)

endpoint = model.deploy(deployed_model_id='sentiment-endpoint')
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you validate labeling quality over time?
- How would you handle class imbalance in multilingual reviews?
- What metrics would you use to detect model drift?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
Assesses ability to design multi-tenant MLOps on GCP with strict isolation, governance, and per-tenant cost controls across regions.

## Key Concepts
- Multi-tenant Resource Isolation (Feature Store namespaces, Model Registry)
- Per-tenant data routing (Pub/Sub, Dataflow)
- Online vs offline feature materialization
- Per-tenant drift monitoring and automatic rollback
- Cost controls (quotas, billing labels, VPC Service Controls)

## Code Example
```javascript
// Pseudo-config: per-tenant resources and routing
const tenantConfig = {
  id: 'tenantA',
  featureStore: 'fs-tenantA',
  modelRegistry: 'registry-tenantA',
  pubsubTopic: 'projects/proj/topics/tenantA-events',
  region: 'us-central1'
}
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you onboard a new tenant with minimal downtime?
- How do you test drift thresholds without impacting production?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
This tests building a practical, auditable end-to-end workflow using GCP ML tools. It emphasizes feature store usage, schema governance, simple drift monitoring, canary deployment, and cost control — all common beginner-to-intermediate patterns.

## Key Concepts
- Vertex AI Feature Store
- Dataflow feature derivation
- Canary deployments in Vertex AI
- Drift and latency monitoring in BigQuery
- Cost-aware scheduling

## Code Example
```python
# Pseudocode for feature derivation
def derive_features(text):
    length = len(text)
    punct = sum(1 for c in text if c in '!?.,')
    cue = sum(1 for w in text.split() if w.lower() in {'great','bad','love','hate'})
    embedding_mean = compute_embedding_mean(text)
    return {'length': length, 'punct': punct, 'cue': cue, 'emb_mean': embedding_mean}
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you test drift thresholds and alerting?
- How would you handle feature drift vs model drift if data distribution shifts?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
This question evaluates practical understanding of data lineage, privacy compliance, and progressive deployment strategies. It moves beyond simple end-to-end piping to assess your grasp of language-specific routing and governance visibility—critical skills for production ML systems.

## Key Concepts
Per-language routing enables specialized model training for each language, improving accuracy. Cloud DLP provides automated PII redaction before data reaches storage, ensuring compliance. Dataset versioning in Cloud Storage maintains reproducibility, while BigQuery lineage tracking provides auditability across the pipeline. Vertex AI supports canary deployments for safe production rollouts, and per-language explainability (via SHAP) combined with drift monitoring ensures model quality over time.

## Architecture Pattern
```javascript
// Dataflow: Split reviews by detected language
reviews
  .apply('Detect Language', detectLanguageTransform())
  .apply('Route by Language', new PartitionIntoBuckets<>(numLanguages));

// Vertex AI: Train per-language model
CustomJobSpec spec = CustomJobSpec.builder()
  .workerPoolSpecs(workerPoolSpecs)
  .parameterValues("language", languageCode)
  .build();
```

questions.explanation:
## Why This Question Is Asked
This question tests advanced GCP ML workflow design, including multi-tenant isolation, cross-tenant knowledge transfer, real-time data pipelines, model explainability, and governance. It evaluates the candidate's ability to design production-grade ML systems that balance data privacy with collaborative learning.

## Key Concepts
- Multi-tenant Feature Store with tenant-separated feature groups and access controls
- Cross-tenant distillation using soft labels from tenant-specific models to improve rare defect detection while preserving isolation via differential privacy
- Canary rollout with percentage-based traffic splitting and performance monitoring
- Vertex Explainable AI with SHAP-based feature attribution for image defect explanations
- Drift monitoring for both prediction distribution (psi-score) and feature skew (ks-test)
- BigQuery provenance logging with pipeline events, model versions, and inference metadata
- Cost ceiling enforcement via Vertex AI Quota API and budget alerts
- Automated rollback triggered by performance degradation (accuracy drop >5%, latency increase >2x)

## Code Example
```python
# TFX Pipeline for Multi-Tenant Image Classification
from tfx.orchestration import pipeline
from tfx.orchestration.kubeflow import kubeflow_dag_runner
from tfx.components import 
    ImportExampleGen, StatisticsGen, SchemaGen, ExampleValidator,
    Transform, Trainer, Evaluator, Pusher

def create_pipeline(project_id: str, region: str, tenant_id: str) -> pipeline.Pipeline:
    """Create TFX pipeline with tenant isolation for manufacturing defect classification."""
    
    # Pub/Sub ingestion via Beam
    example_gen = ImportExampleGen(
        input_base=f'gs://{project_id}-data-{tenant_id}/images/',
        beam_pipeline_args=['--runner=DataflowRunner']
    )
    
    # Dataflow preprocessing with image transformations
    statistics_gen = StatisticsGen(examples=example_gen.outputs['examples'])
    schema_gen = SchemaGen(statistics=statistics_gen.outputs['statistics'])
    example_validator = ExampleValidator(
        statistics=statistics_gen.outputs['statistics'],
        schema=schema_gen.outputs['schema']
    )
    
    # Transform: resize, normalize, extract features for Feature Store
    transform = Transform(
        examples=example_gen.outputs['examples'],
        schema=schema_gen.outputs['schema'],
        module_file='transform.py'
    )
    
    # Feature Store ingestion
    feature_store_config = {
        'project': project_id,
        'location': region,
        'featurestore_id': f'manufacturing-features-{tenant_id}',
        'entity_id': 'image_id',
        'features': [
            {'name': 'image_features', 'value_type': 'BYTES'},
            {'name': 'defect_class', 'value_type': 'INT64'},
            {'name': 'factory_id', 'value_type': 'STRING'}
        ]
    }
    
    # Cross-tenant distillation training
    trainer = Trainer(
        module_file='trainer_distillation.py',
        transformed_examples=transform.outputs['transformed_examples'],
        schema=schema_gen.outputs['schema'],
        transform_graph=transform.outputs['transform_graph'],
        trainer_args={
            'model_uri': f'gs://{project_id}-models/{tenant_id}',
            'distillation_enabled': True,
            'cross_tenant_teacher_uri': f'gs://{project_id}-models/shared-teacher',
            'differential_privacy_epsilon': 1.0
        }
    )
    
    # Vertex Explainable AI: compute SHAP values
    model_blessed = Evaluator(
        examples=example_gen.outputs['examples'],
        model=trainer.outputs['model'],
        eval_config='eval_config.yaml'
    )
    
    # Canary deployment configuration
    canary_config = {
        'canary_percentage': 10,
        'metrics_threshold': {'accuracy': 0.95, 'latency_p99': 200},
        'rollback_on_failure': True
    }
    
    # Pusher to Vertex AI Model Registry
    pusher = Pusher(
        model=trainer.outputs['model'],
        model_blessing=model_blessed.outputs['blessing'],
        push_destination=pusher_pb2.PushDestination(
            vertex_ai_model_registry={
                'project_id': project_id,
                'region': region,
                'model_name': f'defect-classifier-{tenant_id}'
            }
        )
    )
    
    return pipeline.Pipeline(
        pipeline_name=f'manufacturing-defect-pipeline-{tenant_id}',
        pipeline_root=f'gs://{project_id}-pipelines/{tenant_id}',
        components=[
            example_gen, statistics_gen, schema_gen, 
            example_validator, transform, trainer, model_blessed, pusher
        ]
    )

# Drift monitoring with Vertex AI Model Monitoring
def setup_drift_monitoring(project_id: str, region: str, model_name: str):
    """Configure drift alerts for production model."""
    from google.cloud import aiplatform
    
    aiplatform.init(project=project_id, location=region)
    
    model = aiplatform.Model(model_name=f'registry/{model_name}')
    
    # Create monitoring job with drift thresholds
    monitoring_job = aiplatform.ModelDeploymentMonitoringJob.create(
        display_name=f'drift-monitor-{model_name}',
        prediction_drift_detection_config={
            'drift_thresholds': {
                'prediction_drift': {'value': 0.1},  # PSI threshold
                'feature_skew': {'value': 0.05}    # KS-test threshold
            }
        },
        alert_config={'email_alert_config': True}
    )
    
    return monitoring_job

# BigQuery provenance logging
def log_provenance(bigquery_client, run_id: str, tenant_id: str, 
                   pipeline_version: str, model_version: str, cost: float):
    """Log pipeline provenance and cost tracking to BigQuery."""
    table = bigquery_client.dataset('ml_provenance').table('pipeline_runs')
    rows = [{
        'run_id': run_id,
        'tenant_id': tenant_id,
        'pipeline_version': pipeline_version,
        'model_version': model_version,
        'cost_usd': cost,
        'timestamp': 'CURRENT_TIMESTAMP',
        'status': 'COMPLETED'
    }]
    table.insert_rows(rows)

# Cost ceiling enforcement
def check_cost_ceiling(bigquery_client, tenant_id: str, 
                       monthly_budget: float) -> bool:
    """Check if tenant has exceeded cost ceiling; trigger alert if exceeded."""
    query = f"""
    SELECT SUM(cost_usd) as total_cost 
    FROM `ml_provenance.pipeline_runs` 
    WHERE tenant_id = '{tenant_id}' 
    AND DATE_TRUNC(timestamp, MONTH) = DATE_TRUNC(CURRENT_DATE(), MONTH)
    """
    result = bigquery_client.query(query).result()
    total_cost = list(result)[0]['total_cost']
    
    if total_cost >= monthly_budget:
        publish_alert(f'Tenant {tenant_id} exceeded budget: ${total_cost}/${monthly_budget}')
        return False
    return True

# Rollback mechanism
def rollback_model(project_id: str, region: str, tenant_id: str, 
                   target_version: str = 'previous'):
    """Rollback to prior model version on Vertex AI."""
    from google.cloud import aiplatform
    
    aiplatform.init(project=project_id, location=region)
    
    model = aiplatform.Model(f'registry/defect-classifier-{tenant_id}')
    versions = model.version_list()
    
    target = versions[-2] if target_version == 'previous' else target_version
    
    endpoint = aiplatform.Endpoint(
        display_name=f'endpoint-{tenant_id}'
    )
    endpoint.deploy(
        model=model.version(target),
        traffic_percentage=100
    )
    
    log_provenance(bigquery_client, run_id, tenant_id, 
                   f'rollback-to-v{target}', model_version=target, cost=0)
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you implement cost ceiling enforcement per tenant?
- What metrics would trigger an automatic rollback?
- How does differential privacy ensure tenant isolation during distillation?
- What is the expected latency for real-time predictions with explanations?

- **Budget-aware autoscaling**: Regional scaling policies respect cost caps while maintaining throughput and availability

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
Tests ability to design a robust, beginner-friendly end-to-end pipeline with data quality, storage, model training, and monitoring, using core GCP tools.

## Key Concepts
- Data validation in Beam/Dataflow with a JSON schema gate
- Pub/Sub, DLQ and Cloud Storage for invalid data
- BigQuery per-language partitions for scalable analytics
- Vertex AI weekly training and canary endpoint deployments
- Drift alerts by language and simple rollback strategy

## Code Example
```python
import apache_beam as beam
import json

class ValidateMessage(beam.DoFn):
  def process(self, element):
    try:
      msg = json.loads(element)
      if {"review_id","text","language","ts"}.issubset(msg.keys()):
        yield msg
      else:
        raise ValueError
    except Exception:
      yield beam.pvalue.TaggedOutput('invalid', element)
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you test the drift alerts and rollback mechanism? 
- How would you handle schema evolution for new review fields?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
Tests real-time pipeline design, cross-service data governance, and practical cost-control trade-offs in Vertex AI.

## Key Concepts
- Pub/Sub, Dataflow streaming
- Vertex AI Feature Store versioning
- Canary deployment and monitoring
- Drift/latency dashboards in BigQuery
- Data lineage and budget governance

## Code Example
```python
# Dataflow snippet: window by service and emit keyed batches
import apache_beam as beam
from apache_beam.options.pipeline_options import PipelineOptions

class KeyByService(beam.DoFn):
    def process(self, elem):
        yield (elem['service'], elem)

with beam.Pipeline(options=PipelineOptions()) as p:
    (p | 'Read' >> beam.io.ReadFromPubSub(subscription='subs')
       | 'Key' >> beam.ParDo(KeyByService())
       | 'Window' >> beam.WindowInto(beam.window.FixedWindows(60))
       | 'Write' >> beam.io.WriteToText('gs://bucket/raw/')
    )
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you design rollback criteria for the canary model?
- What metrics would you surface to stakeholders and how would you trigger alerts?
